not_found.name: Unknown
description: ## Error
Unable to find the requested arcane school.

abjuration.name: Abjuration
description: ## Abjuration
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/79›
The abjurer uses magic against itself, and masters the art of defensive and warding magics.

::ab[Resistance (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 to an energy type of your choice, chosen when you prepare spells. This resistance can be changed each day." imp11="This resistance increases to 10." imp20="This resistance changes to ‹umr/immunity› to the chosen energy type."}

::ab[Protective Ward (Su)]{l=1 icon=def-protect standard="As a standard action, you can create a 10-foot-radius field of protective magic centered on you that lasts for a number of rounds equal to your Intelligence modifier. All allies in this area (including you) receive a +1 deflection bonus to their AC. This bonus increases by +1 for every five wizard levels you possess." imp5="The bonus increases to +2." imp10="The bonus increases to +3." imp15="The bonus increases to +4." imp20="The bonus increases to +5." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Energy Absorption (Su)]{l=6 icon=def passive="You gain an amount of energy absorption equal to 3 &times& your wizard level per day. Whenever you take energy damage, apply immunity, vulnerability (if any), and resistance first and apply the rest to this absorption, reducing your daily total by that amount. Any damage in excess of your absorption is applied to you normally."}
::h3[Banishment School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/144›
**Associated School:** Abjuration.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *energy absorption* power and the *protective ward* power of the abjuration school.

::ab[Unstable Bonds (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee ability="As a melee touch attack, you can cause a summoned or called creature to become ‹misc/shaken› and ‹misc/staggered› for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1)." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Aura of Banishment (Su)]{l=8 icon=aura standard="You can emit a 30-foot aura of banishment. Any summoned or called creature in the area must make a Will save each round. Once the creature fails a Will saving throw, it is staggered as long as it remains inside the aura. If it fails a second Will saving throw, it is immediately sent back to its home plane and the spell that summoned it immediately ends. If that spell summoned more than one creature, only the creature that failed its saving throws is affected." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Counterspell School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/144›
**Associated School:** Abjuration.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *protective ward* power and the *energy absorption* power of the abjuration school.

::ab[Disruption (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee ability="As a melee touch attack, you can place a field around the target that disrupts spellcasting. While the field is in place, the target must make a ‹rule/concentration› check to cast any spell or to use a spell-like ability in addition to any other required concentration checks. The DC of this check is equal to 15 + twice the spell's level. If the check is failed, the target's spell is wasted. This field lasts for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1)." useMod3=Int}
::ab[Counterspell Mastery-Feat (Su)]{l=6 icon=power passive="You gain ‹feat/Improved Counterspell› as a bonus feat."}

::ab[Counterspell Mastery-Immediate (Su)]{l=6 icon=magic immediate="You may attempt to counterspell an opponent's spell. You must use a spell at least one level higher than the spell being countered to use this ability." useInc="4~wizard~6"}

conjuration.name: Conjuration
description: ## Conjuration
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/80›
The conjurer focuses on the study of summoning monsters and magic alike to bend to his will.

::ab[Summoner's Charm (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="Whenever you cast a conjuration (summoning) spell, increase the duration by a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1)." imp20="You can change the duration of all *summon monster* spells to permanent. You can have no more than one ‹spell/summon monster› spell made permanent in this way at one time. If you designate another *summon monster* spell as permanent, the previous spell immediately ends. This increase is not doubled by ‹feat/Extend Spell›."}

::ab[Acid Dart (Sp)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You can unleash an acid dart targeting any foe within 30 feet as a ranged touch attack. The acid dart deals 1d6 points of acid damage + 1 for every two wizard levels you possess." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Dimensional Steps (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic provokes=No standard="You can use this ability to teleport from one place to another. You can bring other willing creatures with you, but you must expend an equal amount of distance for each additional creature brought with you." usage="You can travel up to 30 feet per wizard level per day. This teleportation must be used in 5-foot increments."}
::h3[Cheliax: Egorian Academy Infernal Binder School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Primer/29›

The prestigious Egorian Academy of the Magical Arts in Cheliax's capital city teaches a full spectrum of magic, both specialized and universalist, but the college is best known for its Infernal Binding program in the school of conjuration. An infernal binder learns the Names of ‹rule/Hell›, and can use those powers to summon and bind ‹family/devil«s› to do her bidding. If she makes a single mistake in the binding, she opens herself to an eternity of torment. Egorian Academy wizards are recognized across Avistan as premier practitioners of the arcane arts. Many hold high positions in magical colleges across Golarion, but most seek to return one day to teach at their alma mater in Egorian.
**Suggested Opposition Schools:** Divination, illusion, or transmutation.
**Alignment:** Lawful Evil, Lawful Neutral, Neutral Evil, or Neutral.
**Requirements:** An infernal binder must choose a familiar as her arcane bond at 1st level.
**Associated School:** Conjuration.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *acid dart* and *dimensional steps* powers of the conjuration school.
::ab[Planar Knowledge (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="As an infernal binder, you gain a +3 bonus on Knowledge (planes) checks."}

::ab[Assume Control (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic standard="You can attempt to gain control over a summoned creature by disrupting the bond between it and the caster who summoned it. You must make a caster level check (1d20 + caster level) equal to 10 + the summoning caster's level. If you know the summoned creature's name, you receive a +2 circumstance bonus on the check. If the check is successful, you can control the summoned creature as if you had summoned it for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1 round). This does not increase the duration of the original summoning. The original summoning caster can attempt to regain control of the summoned creature as a standard action by making a caster level check against your caster level + 10. When your control ends, the creature reverts to the control of its summoner." imp9="You can use this ability to bargain with called creatures as if you were the caster who conjured them." useMod3=Int}
::ab[Imp Familiar (Ex)]{l=7 icon=power passive="You gain ‹feat/Improved Familiar› as a bonus feat, and must take an ‹monster/imp› as a familiar. The imp replaces your current familiar."}
::h3[Creation School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/144›
**Associated School:** Conjuration.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *acid dart* power and the *dimensional steps* power of the conjuration school.

::ab[Create Gear (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic standard="You can create any object that weighs no more than 1 pound per wizard level you possess. The item remains for 1 minute before fading away, although it disappears after one round if it leaves your possession. Creating an item to an exact specification might require a ‹skill/Craft› skill check, subject to GM discretion. The object must be made of simple materials, such as wood, stone, glass, or metal, and cannot contain any moving parts. You could use this ability to create a ‹eq-weapon/dagger›, but not a vial of ‹eq-weapon/alchemist's fire.›" useMod3=Int}

::ab[Creator's Will (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic standard="You can cast ‹spell/minor creation› as a spell-like ability. You can have no more than one minor creation active at a time. If you cast the spell again, the previous casting immediately ends." imp12="This ability improves to ‹spell/major creation›." useInc="2~wizard"}
::h3[Extradimension School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Black Markets/26›
**Associated School:** Conjuration.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school power replaces the *summoner's charm* power of the conjuration school.

::ab[Reshape Space (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="Whenever you cast a spell that creates an extradimensional space or demiplane, increase the duration by a number of units (rounds, minutes, hours, etc.) equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1)." imp20="You can make one spell that creates an extradimensional space or demiplane permanent. You can have no more than one such spell made permanent in this way at a time. If you designate another spell as permanent, the previous permanent spell ends."}
::h3[Teleportation School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/147›
**Associated School:** Conjuration.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school power replaces the *acid dart* power of the conjuration school.

::ab[Shift (Su)]{l=1 icon=power provokes=No swift="You can teleport to a nearby space as if using ‹spell/dimension door›. You must be able to see the space that you are moving into. You cannot take other creatures with you when you use this ability (except for familiars). You can move 5 feet for every two wizard levels you possess (minimum 5 feet)." useMod3=Int}

divination.name: Divination
description: ## Divination
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/80›
Diviners are masters of remote viewing, prophecies, and using magic to explore the world.

::ab[Forewarned (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost-roll passive="You can always act in the surprise round even if you fail to make a Perception roll to notice a foe, but you are still considered ‹rule/flat-footed› until you take an action. In addition, you receive a bonus on initiative checks equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum +1)." imp20="Anytime you roll initiative, assume the roll resulted in a natural 20."}

::ab[Diviner's Fortune (Sp)]{l=1 icon=boost standard="When you activate this school power, you can touch any creature to give it an insight bonus on all of its attack rolls, skill checks, ability checks, and saving throws equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum +1) for 1 round." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Scrying Adept (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost passive="You are always aware when you are being observed via magic, as if you had a permanent ‹spell/detect scrying›. In addition, whenever you ‹spell/scry<ing› on a subject, treat the subject as one step more familiar to you. Very familiar subjects get a -10 penalty on their save to avoid your scrying attempts."}
::h3[Foresight School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/145›
**Associated School:** Divination.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *diviner's fortune* and *scrying adept* powers of the divination school.

::ab[Prescience (Su)]{l=1 icon=roll free="At the beginning of your turn, you may roll a single d20. At any point before your next turn, you may use the result of this roll as the result of any d20 roll you are required to make. If you do not use the d20 result before your next turn, it is lost." useMod3=Int}
:::ab{l=8 icon=boost-lower title="Foretell (Su)" action="Standard Action" useL=wizard}

You can utter a prediction of the immediate future. While your foretelling is in effect, you emit a 30-foot aura of fortune that aids your allies or hinders your enemies, as chosen by you at the time of prediction.
If you choose to aid, you and your allies gain a +2 luck bonus on ability checks, attack rolls, caster level checks, saving throws, and skill checks.
If you choose to hinder, your enemies take a -2 penalty on those rolls instead.
::h3[Prophecy School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Disciple's Doctrine/10›
Prophets who specialize in arcane magic often claim specialty in manipulating the powers of luck to their advantage.
**Associated School:** Divination.
**Replacement Powers:** The following replace the *forewarned* and *scrying adept* powers of the divination school.

::ab[Inspiring Prediction (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost swift="You can predict an ally's success, bolstering others' resolve. You can shout an inspiring prediction, granting each ally within 50 feet who can hear you a +4 luck bonus on her next attack roll, saving throw, or skill check." useMod3=Int}

::ab[In Accordance with the Prophecy (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost standard="You can publicly declare that your next spell is guided by prophecy. When you do, the next spell you cast has a 20% chance of fizzling (1-20 on a d%). If the spell does not fail, treat the spell as if it had been modified by the ‹feat/Empower Spell› feat, even if you do not have that feat." imp12="The chance that the spell fizzles is reduced to 15% (1-15 on a d%)." imp16="The chance is reduced to 10% (1-10 on a d%)." useMod=Int}
::h3[Scryer School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/147›
**Associated School:** Divination.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school power replaces the *diviner's fortune* power of the divination school.

::ab[Send Senses (Sp)]{l=1 icon=magic standard="You place a scrying sensor at a point within medium range (100 feet + 10 feet/wizard level) that you can see and have line of effect to. You can see or hear (not both) through this sensor for number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1). The sensor otherwise functions as a ‹spell/clairaudience/clairvoyance› spell with a caster level equal to your wizard level." useMod3=Int}

enchantment.name: Enchantment
description: ## Enchantment
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/81›
The enchanter uses magic to control and manipulate the minds of his victims.

::ab[Enchanting Smile (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="You gain a +2 enhancement bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, and Intimidate skill checks." imp5="The bonus increases to +3." imp10="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp20="The bonus becomes +6. In addition, whenever you succeed at a saving throw against a spell of the enchantment school, that spell is reflected back at its caster, as per ‹spell/spell turning›."}

::ab[Dazing Touch (Sp)]{l=1 icon=touch standard="You can cause a living creature to become ‹misc/dazed› for 1 round as a melee touch attack. Creatures with more Hit Dice than your wizard level are unaffected." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Aura of Despair (Su)]{l=8 icon=lower standard="You can emit a 30-foot aura of despair. Enemies within this aura take a -2 penalty on ability checks, attack rolls, damage rolls, saving throws, and skill checks." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Controller School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/144›
**Associated School:** Enchantment.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *enchanting smile* and *aura of despair* powers of the enchantment school.

::ab[Force of Will (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-lower passive="You can send thoughts and instructions telepathically to any creature within 60 feet that you have charmed or dominated as though you shared a common language." imp11="Affected creatures can communicate back to you via the telepathic link as well." imp20="Any creature that succeeds at a saving throw against an enchantment spell you have just cast is still affected for 1 round if the spell has a duration greater than 1 round."}

::ab[Irresistible Demand (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic standard="You can force others to do your bidding. As a standard action, you attempt to dominate a creature as though using the ‹spell/dominate monster› spell, except that the creature must have a number of Hit Dice equal to or less than your wizard level. The targeted creature receives a Will saving throw each round to negate the effect. The DC is equal to 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier. You must concentrate as a standard action to maintain control over a dominated creature." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Manipulator School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/146›
**Associated School:** Enchantment.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *dazing touch* and *aura of despair* powers of the enchantment school.

::ab[Beguiling Touch (Sp)]{l=1 icon=touch-magic standard="You can charm a living creature by touching it. Creatures with more Hit Dice than your wizard level are unaffected, as are creatures in combat and those with an attitude of hostile toward you. Creatures receive a Will saving throw to negate the effect. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier. Creatures that fail their save are affected by ‹spell/charm monster› for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1). This is a mind-affecting effect." useMod3=Int}
:::ab{l=8 title="Shape Emotions (Su)" icon=boost-lower action="Standard Action" useL=wizard useNC}
You can emit a 30-foot aura to either ward off or welcome emotional influence.

If you choose to ward, you and your allies within this aura receive a +4 morale bonus on saves against mind-affecting spells and effects, and any fear effects targeting you or your allies are reduced by one step (‹misc/shaken› has no effect, ‹misc/frightened› becomes shaken, and ‹misc/panicked› becomes frightened).
If you chose to enhance emotional influence, enemies within the aura receive a -2 penalty on saves against mind-affecting spells and effects.

evocation.name: Evocation
description: ## Evocation
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/81›
Evokers revel in the raw power of magic, and can use it to create and destroy with shocking ease.

::ab[Intense Spells (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic-roll passive="Whenever you cast an evocation spell that deals hit point damage, add 1/2 your wizard level to the damage (minimum +1). This bonus only applies once to a spell, not once per missile or ray, and cannot be split between multiple missiles or rays. This bonus damage is not increased by ‹feat/Empower Spell› or similar effects. This damage is of the same type as the spell." imp20="Whenever you cast an evocation spell you can roll twice to penetrate a creature's spell resistance and take the better result."}

::ab[Force Missile (Sp)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You can unleash a force missile that automatically strikes a foe, as ‹spell/magic missile›. The force missile deals 1d4 points of damage plus the damage from your intense spells evocation power. This is a force effect." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Elemental Wall (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic ="You can create a wall of energy. This wall deals acid, cold, electricity, or fire damage, determined when you create it. The elemental wall otherwise functions like ‹spell/wall of fire›." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Admixture School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/143›
**Associated School:** Evocation.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *force missile* and *elemental wall* powers of the evocation school.

::ab[Versatile Evocation (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic ability="When you cast an evocation spell that does acid, cold, electricity, or fire damage, you may change the damage dealt to one of the other four energy types. This changes the descriptor of the spell to match the new energy type. Any non-damaging effects remain unchanged unless the new energy type invalidates them (an ‹spell/ice storm› that deals fire damage might still provide a penalty on Perception checks due to smoke, but it would not create difficult terrain). Such effects are subject to GM discretion." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Elemental Manipulation (Su)]{l=8 icon=aura-magic standard="You can emit a 30-foot aura that transforms magical energy. Choose an energy type from acid, cold, electricity, and fire, and a second type to transform it into. Any magical source of energy of this type with a caster level equal to or less than your wizard level is altered to the chosen energy type. This includes supernatural effects from creatures with Hit Dice no greater than your caster level. For example, you could transform a ‹family/white dragon«'s› frigid breath weapon (a supernatural ability), but not a ‹monster/fire elemental«'s› fiery touch (an extraordinary ability). If an effect lies only partially within your aura, only the portions within the aura are transformed." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Generation School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/146›
**Associated School:** Evocation.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *intense spells* and *force missile* powers of the evocation school.

::ab[Lingering Evocations (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost-roll passive="Any evocation spell you cast with a duration greater than instantaneous lasts an additional number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum +1)." imp20="Any dispel checks made against your evocation spells must be rolled twice, and your opponent must use the less favorable result."}

::ab[Wind Servant (Sp)]{l=1 icon=line standard="You can generate a blast of air that hurls an unattended object (or objects) or an object in your possession up to 30 feet in a straight line. If you have a free hand, you can catch an object hurled toward yourself. You can move objects weighing up to 1 pound per wizard level. Objects are not thrown with enough force to cause damage, although fragile objects like alchemical weapons shatter on contact with a creature or hard surface. To hit a creature with an object, you must succeed at a ranged touch attack." useMod3=Int}

illusion.name: Illusion
description: ## Illusion
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/81›
Illusionists use magic to weave confounding images, figments, and phantoms to baffle and vex their foes.

::ab[Extended Illusions (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost ability="Any illusion spell you cast with a duration of &quot&concentration&quot& lasts a number of additional rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level after you stop maintaining concentration (minimum +1 round)." imp20="You can make one illusion spell with a duration of &quot&concentration&quot& become permanent. You can have no more than one illusion made permanent in this way at one time. If you designate another illusion as permanent, the previous permanent illusion ends."}

::ab[Blinding Ray (Sp)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You can fire a shimmering ray at any foe within 30 feet as a ranged touch attack. The ray causes creatures to be ‹misc/blinded› for 1 round. Creatures with more Hit Dice than your wizard level are ‹misc/dazzled› for 1 round instead." useMod3=Int}
::ab[Invisibility Field (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic swift="You can make yourself invisible. This otherwise functions as ‹spell/greater invisibility›." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Deception School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Adventurer's Guide/53›
**Associated School:** Illusion.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *blinding ray* and *extended illusions* powers of the illusion school.

::ab[Deceptive Flourish-Bonus (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="You gain a +2 enhancement bonus on Bluff and Disguise skill checks." imp5="The bonus becomes +3." imp10="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp20="The bonus becomes +6. You also gain the benefits of ‹spell/misdirection› (Will DC = 20 + your Intelligence modifier) or ‹spell/nondetection› permanently. At will as a standard action, you can change which of the two spells is active, and each time you activate *misdirection,* you can choose a new creature or object as the secondary object."}

::ab[Subtle Misdirection (Sp)]{l=1 icon=protect standard="You can touch a creature or an object that weighs no more than 25 pounds per caster level to impart an illusory ward that lasts for 1 minute. Attacks against a warded creature suffer a 20% miss chance, and the ward ends once it negates an attack. The DC of any caster level check, skill check, or saving throw to detect or determine the properties of a warded item increases by 1, plus 1 for every 5 wizard levels you have." useMod3=Int}
::h3[Phantasm School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/146›
**Associated School:** Illusion.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *blinding ray* and *invisibility field* powers of the illusion school.

::ab[Terror (Su)]{l=1 icon=touch standard="You can make a melee touch attack that causes a creature to be assailed by nightmares only it can see. The creature provokes an attack of opportunity from you or an ally of your choice. Creatures with more Hit Dice than your wizard level are unaffected. This is a mind-affecting fear effect." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Bedeviling Aura (Su)]{l=8 icon=lower standard="You can emit a 30-foot aura that bedevils your enemies with phantasmal assailants. Enemies within this aura move at half speed, are unable to take attacks of opportunity, and are considered to be ‹rule/flanked›. This is a mind-affecting effect." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Qadira: Mage of the Veil School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Primer/29›

The city of Katheer is home to a great number of arcane colleges in the Qadiran tradition. Magic has a proud history in this land; its many schools compete with one another to find the best route to understanding the secrets of power.

Among these is the focused school of veil magic. Rather than controlling the elements or transforming the environment, mages of the veil focus on much more subtle magic - blending into their surroundings to watch, observe, and gather secrets, or fading from view completely for the sake of solitude. Most graduates of this college enter the service of the satrap as spies, scouts, or infiltrators.
**Requirements:** A mage of the veil must select ‹spell/disguise self› as one of his spells known at 1st level.
**Class Skills:** A mage of the veil gains Bluff, Disguise, and Stealth as class skills.
**Associated School:** Illusion.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *blinding ray* and *invisibility field* powers of the illusion school.
:::ab{l=1 icon=magic title="Face in the Crowd (Sp)" action="Standard Action" useL=wizard useUnit=minute useNC="1-minute"}

You can cause yourself to appear so normal, mundane, and unexceptional that you blend in to your surroundings. All creatures within 30 feet treat you as if you belonged there, effectively ignoring you. Creatures outside of this area may notice that you appear out of place, but as soon as they approach within 30 feet they are affected by the magic and no longer think something is amiss. If you interact with an affected creature in any way, it gains a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) to disbelieve the illusion and notice you. This is a mind-affecting phantasm effect.

This ability does not turn you invisible and opponents are not considered ‹rule/flat-footed›. It does not allow you to disguise yourself as a specific individual, type of person, or member of another race, so you cannot blend into a group of uniformed guards or appear as a member of an individual's family, and a human cannot blend into a group of ‹monster/gnoll«s›. You can blend in with any group made up of different types of people, such as a crowd of courtiers in a king's throne room, and you can disguise yourself (either through magic or more mundane means) to appear as a specific type of person or another race (such as a uniformed guard or a gnoll) before using this ability.

::ab[Master of the Veil (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic standard="You can create an illusion that hides the appearance of yourself and any number of allies within 30 feet. This ability otherwise functions like the spell ‹spell/veil›." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Shadow School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/147›
**Associated School:** Illusion.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *blinding ray* and *invisibility field* powers of the illusion school.

::ab[Binding Darkness (Sp)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You cast a weave of shadows at any foe within 30 feet as a ranged touch attack. The shadows ‹rule/entangle<d› your foe for 1 round. In conditions of bright light, this duration is halved (minimum 1 round). A creature entangled by your shadows has ‹rule/concealment› from those without darkvision or the ability to see in darkness, and other creatures likewise have concealment relative to it." imp5="The base duration becomes 2 rounds." imp10="The base duration becomes 3 rounds." imp15="The base duration becomes 4 rounds." imp20="The base duration becomes 5 rounds." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Shadow Step (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic provokes=No standard="You can use this ability to walk through the ‹rule/Shadow Plane› and reappear. You can travel either in a single round or broken up across multiple shadow steps. Travel through the Shadow Plane is imprecise; when you arrive, you re-enter 1 square off target, as per the rules for ‹rule/thrown »splash weapon«s›. If this would place you in an occupied square, you instead arrive in the nearest safe location. When you arrive, you are cloaked in shadow and gain concealment as the ‹spell/blur› spell for 1 round. You may bring other willing creatures with you, but you must expend an equal amount of distance for each additional creature brought with you. They likewise re-enter off target (roll location for each creature) and are cloaked in shadow for 1 round." usage="You have 30 feet of travel per wizard level you can use per day. This movement must be used in 5-foot increments."}

necromancy.name: Necromancy
description: ## Necromancy
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/81›
The dread and feared necromancer commands undead and uses the foul power of unlife against his enemies.

::ab[Power over Undead (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic-lower standard="You receive ‹feat/Command Undead› or ‹feat/Turn Undead› as a bonus feat, and you can ‹ability/channel energy›, but only to use the selected feat. You can take other feats to add to this ability, such as ‹feat/Extra Channel› and ‹feat/Improved Channel›, but not feats that alter this ability, such as ‹feat/Elemental Channel› and ‹feat/Alignment Channel›. The DC to save against these feats is equal to 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Charisma modifier." imp20="Undead cannot add their channel resistance to the save against this ability." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Grave Touch (Sp)]{l=1 icon=touch standard="You can make a melee touch attack that causes a living creature to become ‹misc/shaken› for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1). If you touch a shaken creature with this ability, it becomes ‹misc/frightened› for 1 round if it has fewer Hit Dice than your wizard level." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Life Sight (Su)]{l=8 icon=power passive="You gain ‹umr/blindsight› to a range of 10 feet for a number of rounds per day equal to your wizard level. This ability only allows you to detect living creatures and undead creatures. This sight also tells you whether a creature is living or undead. Constructs and other creatures that are neither living nor undead cannot be seen with this ability." useL=wizard useNC imp12="The range increases to 20 feet." imp16="The range increases to 30 feet." imp20="The range increases to 40 feet."}
::h3[Life School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/146›
**Associated School:** Necromancy.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *power over undead* and *grave touch* powers of the necromancy school.

::ab[Healing Grace (Su)]{l=1 icon=aid ability="Whenever you cast a spell that has targets, affects creatures in an area, or requires an attack roll, you may heal creatures affected by the spell a total of 1 point of damage per level of the spell. This healing may be spread out between the targets of the spell in any way you choose. This healing occurs when the spell is cast and has no effect on creatures that enter its area after the spell is in place. If you assign any of the healing to an undead creature, it instead takes 1 point of damage for each point assigned." imp11="The amount of damage cured increases to 2 points of damage per level of the spell." imp20="The amount of damage cured increases to 3 points of damage per level of the spell."}

::ab[Share Essence (Sp)]{l=1 icon=aid standard="You can share your vital energy with a living creature that you touch. You take 1d6 points of nonlethal damage + 1 for every two wizard levels you possess. You cannot take an amount of nonlethal damage equal to or greater than your current hit point total; any excess is prevented. The recipient gains a number of ‹rule/temporary hit points› equal to the amount of damage you received (prevented damage is not counted). These temporary hit points disappear 1 hour later. You may not use this ability to grant yourself temporary hit points." useMod3=Int}
::h3[Undead School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/147›
**Associated School:** Necromancy.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school power replaces the *grave touch* power of the necromancy school.

::ab[Bolster (Sp)]{l=1 icon=boost standard="You can touch an undead creature and infuse it with negative energy. It gains a +1 profane bonus on all attack rolls and saving throws, as well as 1 temporary hit point per Hit Die and a +2 bonus to its ‹umr/channel resistance›. These bonuses last for a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1 round). The bonuses and temporary hit points are immediately dispelled if the creature is within the area of a ‹spell/consecrate› spell." imp5="The bonus on attack rolls and saving throws increases to +2." imp10="The bonus on attack rolls and saving throws increases to +3." imp15="The bonus on attack rolls and saving throws increases to +4." imp20="The bonus on attack rolls and saving throws increases to +5." useMod3=Int}

transmutation.name: Transmutation
description: ## Transmutation
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/82›
Transmuters use magic to change the world around them.

::ab[Physical Enhancement (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="You gain a +1 enhancement bonus to one physical ability score (Strength, Dexterity, or Constitution). You can change this bonus to a new ability score when you prepare spells." imp5="The bonus becomes +2." imp10="The bonus becomes +3." imp15="The bonus becomes +4." imp20="The bonus becomes +5. This bonus applies to two physical ability scores of your choice."}

::ab[Telekinetic Fist (Sp)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You can strike with a telekinetic fist, targeting any foe within 30 feet as a ranged touch attack. The telekinetic fist deals 1d4 points of bludgeoning damage + 1 for every two wizard levels you possess." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Change Shape (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic standard="You can change your shape, functioning like ‹spell/beast shape II› or ‹spell/elemental body I›." imp12="This ability functions like ‹spell/beast shape III› or ‹spell/elemental body II›." useL=wizard useNC}
:::aside
At 5th, 10th, 15th, and 20th levels, a transmuter may learn an ‹ability/annointing<s› instead of gaining a wizard bonus feat.
::h3[Enhancement School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/145›
**Associated School:** Transmutation.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *telekinetic fist* and *change shape* powers of the transmutation school.

::ab[Augment (Sp)]{l=1 icon=boost standard="You can touch a creature and grant it either a +2 enhancement bonus to a single ability score of your choice or a +1 bonus to natural armor that stacks with any natural armor the creature might possess. This augmentation lasts a number of rounds equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1 round)." imp5="The natural armor bonus becomes +2." imp10="The enhancement bonus to one ability score increases to +4, and the natural armor bonus becomes +3." imp15="The natural armor bonus becomes +4." imp20="The natural armor bonus becomes +5." useMod3=Int}
::ab[Perfection of Self (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost swift="You can grant yourself an enhancement bonus to a single ability score equal to 1/2 your wizard level (maximum +10) for one round." useL=wizard}
::h3[Shapechange School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/147›
**Associated School:** Transmutation.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school power replaces the *telekinetic fist* power of the transmutation school.

::ab[Battleshaping (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee swift="As a swift action, you grow a single ‹umr/natural weapon›. The natural weapon lasts for 1 round. You can grow a claw, a bite, or a gore attack. These attacks deal the normal damage for a creature of your size. At 11th level, you can shape two natural weapons. You may not grow additional limbs or a tail with this ability." imp4="Your natural weapon gains a +1 enhancement bonus on attack and damage rolls." imp8="The bonus becomes +2." imp11="You can shape two natural weapons." imp12="The bonus becomes +3." imp16="The bonus becomes +4." imp20="The bonus becomes +5." useMod3=Int}

universalist.name: Universalist
description: ## Universalist
‹SOURCE PRPG Core Rulebook/82›
Wizards who do not specialize (known as as universalists) have the most diversity of all arcane spellcasters.

::ab[Hand of the Apprentice (Su)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You cause your melee weapon to fly from your grasp and strike a foe before instantly returning to you. You can make a single attack using a melee weapon at a range of 30 feet. This attack is treated as a ranged attack with a thrown weapon, except that you add your Intelligence modifier on the attack roll instead of your Dexterity modifier (damage still relies on Strength). This ability cannot be used to perform a combat maneuver." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Metamagic Mastery (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic ability="You can apply any one metamagic feat that you know to a spell you are about to cast. This does not alter the level of the spell or the casting time. Any time you use this ability to apply a metamagic feat that increases the spell level by more than 1, you must use an additional daily usage for each level above 1 that the feat adds to the spell. Even though this ability does not modify the spell's actual level, you cannot use this ability to cast a spell whose modified spell level would be above the level of the highest-level spell that you are capable of casting." useInc="2~wizard~8"}
::h3[Absalom: Arcanamirium Crafter School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Primer/28›

The most famous magical college of the Inner Sea region, the Arcanamirium of Absalom teaches a broad array of specialties, but its most intensive specialty is no specialization at all. It is one of the primary training academies of universalist magic on Golarion, and the wizards who graduate from the school become well respected, well paid, and highly sought-after in their homelands. An Arcanamirium crafter is one of the builders of the arcane world, assembling magic items with facility, skill, and power. He is intimately familiar with the craft and practice of imbuing items with arcane power, and works with precision even early in his career to help create some of the most powerful magic items of Golarion.
**Associated School:** Universalist.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school power replaces the *hand of the apprentice* power of the universalist school.

::ab[Metacharge (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power-boost passive="As an Arcanamirium crafter, you gain a bonus feat, which must be an ‹main/item creation feat› or ‹main/metamagic feat›. You must still meet all prerequisites for a bonus feat, including caster level minimums. When using metamagic feats to create magic items, your skill and understanding of the principles involved in the item's creation give you a +2 bonus on the skill check made to create the item."}

aether.name: Aether
description: ## Aether
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/20›

Aether is a strange and complex substance that occurs where the Elemental Planes touch the ‹rule/Ethereal Plane›. Wizards who study aether use it to manipulate objects on the Material Plane. The aether elemental school is an elemental arcane school a wizard can specialize in instead of one of the standard schools of magic.

Like a normal arcane school, the aether elemental school grants a number of school powers, as well as one bonus spell slot of each level the wizard can cast, from 1st on up. This bonus spell slot must be used to prepare a spell from the aether elemental school's spell list. Unlike a normal arcane school, the aether elemental school requires the wizard to select a single element as his opposition school (air, earth, fire, or water, for example). The wizard does not need to select a second opposition school. He must expend two spell slots to prepare a spell from his opposed elemental school as normal.

::ab[Aether Magic]{icon=learn s0="Telekinetic projectile" s2="Spiritual weapon" s4="Spiritual ally" s5="Ethereal envelope" s6="Animate objects" s7="Ectoplasmic eruption" s9="Telekinetic storm" note="Add these spells to your wizard spell list at the listed spell level."}

::ab[Aether Supremacy (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost-power-roll passive="You gain a +2 enhancement bonus on Sleight of Hand checks. In addition, you can cast ‹spell/mage hand› at will as a spell-like ability that uses your caster level." imp5="The enhancement bonus increases to +3, and you can attempt Sleight of Hand checks against a target within 30 feet, though the skill check DC increases by 5, and any creature within line of sight of either you or the target can attempt an opposed Perception check to notice the attempt." imp10="The enhancement bonus increases to +4, and you can attempt a Sleight of Hand check as a move action with only a -10 penalty." imp15="The enhancement bonus increases to +5." imp20="The enhancement bonus increases to +6, and whenever you attempt a Sleight of Hand check, assume the roll resulted in a natural 20."}

::ab[Telekinetic Protection (Su)]{l=1 icon=def swift="You can grant yourself protection against nonmagical attacks by forming a field to telekinetically deflect them. You gain DR 5/magic for 1 round." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Receptive Vibrations (Su)]{l=8 icon=def-boost passive="You are constantly surrounded by ephemeral and invisible strands of aether, increasing your awareness of your surroundings. You are never denied your Dexterity bonus to AC against attacks made within 30 feet of you because you are flat-footed or because your assailant is unseen. In addition, you gain a bonus on Perception checks to avoid being surprised equal to half your wizard level."}
::h3[Aether Spell List]{jl}
- 0-*telekinetic projectile*
- 1st-‹spell/floating disk›, ‹spell/magic missile›, ‹spell/unseen servant›
- 2nd-‹spell/blur›, ‹spell/pilfering hand›, *spiritual weapon*
- 3rd-‹spell/ablative barrier›, ‹spell/blink›, ‹spell/ectoplasmic snare›, ‹spell/force punch›, ‹spell/tiny hut›
- 4th-‹spell/resilient sphere›, *spiritual ally,* ‹spell/telekinetic charge›, ‹spell/telekinetic maneuver›
- 5th-‹spell/condensed ether›, *ethereal envelope,* ‹spell/interposing hand›, ‹spell/mage's faithful hound›, ‹spell/secret chest›, ‹spell/telekinesis›, ‹spell/wall of ectoplasm›
- 6th-*animate objects,* ‹spell/enemy hammer›, ‹spell/forceful hand›, ‹spell/leashed shackles›
- 7th-*ectoplasmic eruption,* ‹spell/ethereal jaunt›, ‹spell/grasping hand›, ‹spell/phase door›
- 8th-‹spell/clenched fist›, ‹spell/telekinetic sphere›
- 9th-‹spell/etherealness›, *telekinetic storm*

air.name: Air
description: ## Air
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/142›
The air elementalist uses the forces of the wind, sky, clouds, and lightning to confuse and destroy his foes, all while flying through the air with ease.

::ab[Air Supremacy (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost-power-roll passive="You gain a +2 enhancement bonus on Fly skill checks. In addition, you can cast ‹spell/feather fall› on yourself at will." imp5="The bonus becomes +3, and you can cast ‹spell/levitate› on yourself at will." imp10="The bonus becomes +4, and you can cast ‹spell/fly› on yourself at will." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp20="The bonus becomes +6, and whenever you make a Fly skill check, assume the roll resulted in a natural 20."}

::ab[Lightning Flash (Su)]{l=1 icon=aura standard="You can unleash a flash of electricity. This flash deals 1d6 points of electricity damage + 1 point for every two wizard levels you possess to all creatures within 5 feet of you and ‹misc/dazzle«s› them for 1d4 rounds. A successful Reflex save negates the dazzled effect and halves the damage. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Cyclone (Su)]{l=8 icon=aura standard="You can create a vortex of turbulent wind around yourself. This vortex has a radius of up to 10 feet and a maximum height of 10 feet per wizard level you possess. Any ranged attack passing through this vortex automatically misses. Any flying creature moving through the vortex must make a Fly skill check or immediately fall from the sky, taking falling damage (the creature cannot make a Fly check to reduce or negate this damage). Creatures on the ground cannot pass through the vortex without first making a Strength check. The DC of this check is equal to 10 + your caster level. The vortex is faintly visible and can be spotted with a DC 15 Perception skill check." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Ice School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Air or Water.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *lightning flash* and *cyclone* powers of the air school.

::ab[Freezing Shards (Su)]{l=1 icon=aura standard="You can cause shards of ice to burst forth from the ground around you. The shards deal 1d4 points of piercing damage and 1d6 points of cold damage + 1 point for every 2 wizard levels you have to all creatures within 5 feet of you. A successful Reflex save (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) negates the piercing damage and halves the cold damage. The shards remain until the beginning of your next turn; during this time, the area is ‹rule/difficult terrain›. You are not affected by your own freezing shards and can move through the area normally." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Icy Shackles (Su)]{l=8 icon=melee standard="You can make a melee touch attack against a foe to conjure icy restraints around its ankles, reducing its base speed to 5 feet and dealing it an amount of cold damage at the beginning of its turn equal to your Intelligence modifier. The manacles have AC 10, hardness 0, and 30 hit points, and a creature can destroy them with a successful Strength check (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) as a standard action. The icy shackles melt away after 1 minute." useMod=Int}
::h3[Smoke School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Air or Fire.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *lightning flash* and *cyclone* powers of the air school.

::ab[Smokesight (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-boost standard="You can constantly see normally through natural fog and smoke, as well as any fog and smoke that you (but not others) magically create. You can choose to touch another creature to grant it this vision for 1 hour." useMod3=Int}
::ab[Body of Smoke (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic swift="You can assume a ‹spell/gaseous form› (as per the spell of that name)." useL=wizard useUnit=minute useNC="1-minute"}
::h3[Air Spells]{jl}
- 0-‹spell/message›
- 1st-‹spell/alter winds›, *feather fall,* ‹spell/shocking grasp›
- 2nd-‹spell/elemental speech›, ‹spell/elemental touch›, ‹spell/glide›, ‹spell/gust of wind›, *levitate,* ‹spell/resist energy›, ‹spell/summon monster II›, ‹spell/whispering wind›
- 3rd-‹spell/cloak of winds›, ‹spell/draconic reservoir›, ‹spell/elemental aura›, *fly, gaseous form,* ‹spell/lightning bolt›, ‹spell/protection from energy›, ‹spell/wind wall›
- 4th-‹spell/ball lightning›, ‹spell/detonate›, ‹spell/dragon's breath›, ‹spell/elemental body I›, ‹spell/river of wind›, ‹spell/shout›, ‹spell/summon monster IV›
- 5th-‹spell/cone of cold›, ‹spell/elemental body II›, ‹spell/geyser›, ‹spell/overland flight›, ‹spell/planar adaptation›, ‹spell/lesser planar binding›, ‹spell/suffocation›, ‹spell/summon monster V›
- 6th-‹spell/chain lightning›, ‹spell/elemental body III›, ‹spell/planar binding›, ‹spell/sirocco›, ‹spell/summon monster VI›
- 7th-‹spell/control weather›, ‹spell/elemental body IV›, ‹spell/mass fly›, ‹spell/mass planar adaptation›, ‹spell/summon monster VII›
- 8th-‹spell/greater planar binding›, ‹spell/greater shout›, ‹spell/stormbolts›, ‹spell/summon monster VIII›
- 9th-‹spell/gate›, ‹spell/mass suffocation›, ‹spell/winds of vengeance›

earth.name: Earth
description: ## Earth
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/142›
The earth elementalist draws power from the stone around him, shaping it, shattering it, and bending it to his will. He can use it to defend himself or cause it to rise up and crush his foes.

::ab[Earth Supremacy (Su)]{l=1 icon=def-melee-power passive="You gain a +2 enhancement bonus to your CMD to resist ‹rule/bull rush›, ‹rule/reposition›, ‹rule/trip›, and ‹rule/overrun› attempts as long as you are touching the ground. In addition, you gain a +1 insight bonus on melee attack and damage rolls whenever both you and your foe are touching the ground." imp5="The enhancement bonus becomes +3." imp10="The enhancement bonus becomes +4." imp15="The enhancement bonus becomes +5." imp20="The enhancement bonus becomes +6. In addition, earth and stone do not block the line of effect of your spells, although they do still block your line of sight."}

::ab[Acid Cloud (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic standard="You can create a 5-foot-radius cloud of acid vapor within 30 feet that lasts for 1 round. This cloud deals 1d6 points of acid damage + 1 point for every two wizard levels you possess to all creatures in the cloud and ‹misc/sicken«s› them for 1 round. A successful Fortitude save negates the sickened effect and halves the damage. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier. Creatures that begin their turn inside the cloud can move out that turn without penalty, but those that enter the cloud are affected." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Earth Glide (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="You gain the ability to move through earth, dirt, and stone. You cannot move through worked earth or stone; only natural substances can be traversed. If your total duration expires before you exit the earth, you are flung back to the point where you entered the stone, take 4d6 points of damage, and are ‹misc/stunned› for 1 round. Your burrowing does not leave a hole, nor does it give any sign of your presence (although you can be detected by creatures with ‹umr/tremorsense›)." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Magma School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Earth or Fire.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *acid cloud* and *earth glide* powers of the earth school.

::ab[Magma Furrow (Su)]{l=1 icon=line standard="You can send forth a 20-foot line of magma. Any creature in this line takes 1d6 points of fire damage + 1 point for every 2 wizard levels you have. A successful Reflex save (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) halves this damage. In addition, the ground in this area remains molten for another 1d4 rounds. Creatures that begin their turns in the area can move out that turn without penalty, but those that enter the area each take 1d6 points of fire damage." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Lava Shield (Su)]{l=8 icon=def standard="You can create a barrier of lava to protect you for 1 round. Choose one edge of your space. That edge is treated as a solid wall for attacks targeting you only. You gain total ‹rule/cover› from attacks that pass through this edge, and you gain the benefits of ‹spell/fire shield› (warm shield) while adjacent to the lava shield. You can dismiss the lava shield as a free action, and it disappears immediately if you leave your space." useInc="2~wizard" useUnit=round}
::h3[Mud School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Earth or Water.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *acid cloud* and *earth glide* powers of the earth school.

::ab[Hindering Muck (Su)]{l=1 icon=cone standard="You can create a 15-foot cone of sticky mud. Any creature in the cone must succeed at a Reflex save (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) or become ‹rule/entangled› for 1 round for every wizard level you have. An affected creature can take a standard action that provokes attacks of opportunity to wipe off enough of the mud to remove the entangled condition. Jumping into a large body of water or being subject to an effect that creates a substantial amount of water (such as a casting of ‹spell/create water›) removes all the mud on a creature." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Mud Missile (Su)]{l=8 icon=zap standard="You can fling a ball of hardened mud at a foe within 30 feet. If you succeed at a ranged touch attack, the mud slams into the target's face, dealing 1d6 points of bludgeoning damage for every 2 wizard levels you have and ‹misc/blind«ing› the target for 1d4 rounds. A creature can remove the blinding mud from itself or another creature with a successful Strength check (DC = 10 + half your wizard level) as a standard action." useInc="2~wizard"}
::h3[Earth Spells]{jl}
- 0-‹spell/acid splash›
- 1st-‹spell/expeditious excavation›, ‹spell/grease›, ‹spell/stone fist›

- 2nd-‹spell/acid arrow›, ‹spell/create pit›, ‹spell/elemental speech›, ‹spell/glitterdust›, ‹spell/elemental touch›, ‹spell/resist energy›, ‹spell/shatter›, ‹spell/stone call›, ‹spell/summon monster II›
- 3rd-‹spell/draconic reservoir›, ‹spell/elemental aura›, ‹spell/protection from energy›, ‹spell/shifting sand›, ‹spell/spiked pit›, ‹spell/stinking cloud›
- 4th-‹spell/acid pit›, ‹spell/calcific touch›, ‹spell/detonate›, ‹spell/dragon's breath›, ‹spell/elemental body I›, ‹spell/stone shape›, ‹spell/stoneskin›, ‹spell/summon monster IV›

- 5th-‹spell/cone of cold›, ‹spell/elemental body II›, ‹spell/geyser›, ‹spell/hungry pit›, ‹spell/passwall›, ‹spell/planar adaptation›, ‹spell/lesser planar binding›, ‹spell/summon monster V›, ‹spell/transmute mud to rock›, ‹spell/transmute rock to mud›, ‹spell/wall of stone›
- 6th-‹spell/acid fog›, ‹spell/elemental body III›, ‹spell/flesh to stone›, ‹spell/move earth›, ‹spell/planar binding›, ‹spell/stone to flesh›, ‹spell/summon monster VI›, ‹spell/wall of iron›
- 7th-‹spell/elemental body IV›, ‹spell/mass planar adaptation›, ‹spell/rampart›, ‹spell/reverse gravity›, ‹spell/statue›, ‹spell/summon monster VII›
- 8th-‹spell/iron body›, ‹spell/greater planar binding›, ‹spell/summon monster VIII›, ‹spell/wall of lava›
- 9th-‹spell/clashing rocks›, ‹spell/gate›, ‹spell/world wave›

fire.name: Fire
description: ## Fire
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/142›
The fire elementalist sees a world around him that is made to burn, and he can bring that fire to consume his foes. He has also learned that fire can purify and protect, if properly controlled.

::ab[Fire Supremacy (Su)]{l=1 icon=def passive="You gain ‹umr/resistance› 5 to fire." imp10="This resistance increases to 10." imp20="You gain ‹umr/immunity› to fire damage. In addition, whenever you are within 5 feet of a source of flame at least as large as a campfire, you can draw the fire around you for 1 round as a swift action. Anyone striking you with a melee weapon or unarmed strike takes an amount of fire damage equal to 1/2 your wizard level (minimum 1). Weapons with reach avoid this damage."}

::ab[Fire Jet (Su)]{l=1 icon=line standard="You can send forth a 20-foot line of fire. Anyone in this line takes 1d6 points of fire damage + 1 point for every two wizard levels you possess. A successful Reflex save halves this damage. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier. Creatures that fail their saving throw ‹rule/catch fire› and take 1d6 points of fire damage on the following round. Creatures that catch fire can avoid this damage by taking a full-round action to extinguish the flames by making a DC 15 Reflex save. Rolling on the ground gives a +2 circumstance bonus on the save. Dousing the creature with water automatically extinguishes the flame." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Dancing Flame (Su)]{l=8 icon=magic standard="You can sculpt fire to suit your desires. With one use of this ability you can move any nonmagical fire up to 30 feet. Alternatively you can use this ability (as a free action) to alter any fire spell that you cast with a duration of instantaneous by removing any number of squares from its area of affect. If the fire spell has a duration, you can use this ability to reposition the spell, within its original range (treat this as if you had just cast the spell, even though the duration is unchanged). You cannot use this ability on a fire spell that you did not cast. If you move a nonmagical flame, it must have a new source of fuel. If it does not, it is extinguished in one round. You can use this ability a number of times per day equal to 1/2 your wizard level."}
::h3[Magma School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Earth or Fire.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *fire jet* and *dancing flame* powers of the fire school.

::ab[Magma Furrow (Su)]{l=1 icon=line standard="You can send forth a 20-foot line of magma. Any creature in this line takes 1d6 points of fire damage + 1 point for every 2 wizard levels you have. A successful Reflex save (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) halves this damage. In addition, the ground in this area remains molten for another 1d4 rounds. Creatures that begin their turns in the area can move out that turn without penalty, but those that enter the area each take 1d6 points of fire damage." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Lava Shield (Su)]{l=8 icon=def standard="You can create a barrier of lava to protect you for 1 round. Choose one edge of your space. That edge is treated as a solid wall for attacks targeting you only. You gain total ‹rule/cover› from attacks that pass through this edge, and you gain the benefits of ‹spell/fire shield› (warm shield) while adjacent to the lava shield. You can dismiss the lava shield as a free action, and it disappears immediately if you leave your space." useInc="2~wizard" useUnit=round}
::h3[Smoke School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Air or Fire.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *fire jet* and *dancing flame* powers of the fire school.

::ab[Smokesight (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-boost standard="You can constantly see normally through natural fog and smoke, as well as any fog and smoke that you (but not others) magically create. You can choose to touch another creature to grant it this vision for 1 hour." useMod3=Int}
::ab[Body of Smoke (Sp)]{l=8 icon=magic swift="You can assume a ‹spell/gaseous form› (as per the spell of that name)." useL=wizard useUnit=minute useNC="1-minute"}

metal.name: Metal
description: ## Metal
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/87›

Metal represents firmness, rigidity, persistence, strength, determination, and electricity. It is commonly represented by gold and silver as its purest forms, though lower magic relating to iron and steel is part of the metal school.
::ab[Metal Magic]{icon=learn s3="Chill metal~~heat metal" s5="Rusting grasp" s9="Repel metal or stone" note="Add these spells to your wizard spell list at the listed spell level."}

::ab[Metal Rending (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic-roll passive="Whenever you cast a spell that deals hit point damage, you deal extra damage to creatures made of metal or wearing medium or heavy metal armor. Affected creatures take additional damage equal to 1/2 your wizard level. This bonus damage is not increased by ‹feat/Empower Spell› or similar effects. This bonus only applies once to a spell, not once per missile or ray, and cannot be split between multiple missiles or rays. The damage is of the same type as the spell." imp20="Whenever you cast a spell on a creature made of metal or wearing medium or heavy armor, you can roll twice to penetrate the creature's spell resistance and take the better result."}

::ab[Iron Skin (Su)]{l=1 icon=def standard="You gain a +2 armor bonus to your Armor Class for 10 minutes." imp5="The bonus becomes +3." imp10="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp20="The bonus becomes +6." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Shrapnel Burst (Su)]{l=8 icon=aura swift="You can cause jagged pieces of metal to explode outward from your body. These shards deal 1d6 points of piercing damage per two wizard levels (minimum 1d6) to all creatures within a 10-foot-radius ‹misc/burst›. A Reflex save halves this damage. In addition, the twisted scraps of metal make the area ‹rule/difficult terrain› until your next turn (at which time they disappear)." imp10="The shrapnel bypasses damage reduction as if it were a magic weapon." useInc="5~wizard~8"}
::h3[Metal Spells]{jl}

- 1st-‹spell/gravity bow›, ‹spell/magic weapon›, ‹spell/shocking grasp›

- 4th-‹spell/malfunction›, ‹spell/shout›, ‹spell/stoneskin›
- 5th-‹spell/lightning arc›, ‹spell/major creation›, ‹spell/rapid repair›, *rusting grasp,* ‹spell/soothe construct›, ‹spell/unbreakable construct›

- 7th-‹spell/control construct›, ‹spell/statue›

- 9th-‹spell/meteor swarm›, *repel metal or stone,* ‹spell/ride the lightning›

void.name: Void
description: ## Void
‹SOURCE Dragon Empires Primer/29›

The void is a complex idea considered by some to be a fifth element, one that comprises thought, space, spiritualism, and insight. Wizards who tap into its mysterious powers gain control over energies that bind the earth to the heavens and the planes to their mysterious and eternal alignments, as well as the powers that stitch living beings to their spirits. The void elemental school presented here is an additional arcane school for the wizard class.

A wizard who specializes in the void element gains a number of school powers and one bonus spell slot of each level that the wizard can cast, from 1st on up. This bonus spell slot must be used to prepare a spell from the void elemental school's spell list. Unlike a normal arcane school, the void elemental school requires the wizard to select a single element (air, earth, fire, or water) as his opposition school. A wizard must expend two spell slots to prepare a spell from his opposed elemental school as normal. He does not need to select a second opposition school.

::ab[Void Magic]{icon=learn s1="Keen senses" s3="Twilight knife" s4="Wandering star motes" s6="True seeing" s8="Moment of prescience" note="Add these spells to your wizard spell list at the listed spell level."}

::ab[Void Awareness (Su)]{l=1 icon=def-roll passive="Your ability to recognize the void allows your body to react to magical manifestations before you're even aware of them. You gain a +2 insight bonus on saving throws against spells and spell-like abilities." imp5="The bonus becomes +3." imp10="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp20="The bonus becomes +6. In addition, whenever you would be affected by a spell or spell-like ability that allows a saving throw, you can roll twice to save against the effect and take the better result."}

::ab[Reveal Weakness (Su)]{l=1 icon=lower standard="Select a foe within 30 feet. That creature takes a penalty to its AC and on saving throws equal to 1/2 your caster level (minimum -1) for 1 round." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Aura of Prescience (Su)]{l=8 icon=boost standard="You can emit a 30-foot aura of void energy. Allies within this aura gain a +2 insight bonus on ability checks, attack rolls, damage rolls, saving throws, and skill checks." useL=wizard useNC}
::h3[Void Spells]{jl}

- 1st-‹spell/gravity bow›, *keen senses,* ‹spell/shield›, ‹spell/true strike›

- 3rd-‹spell/arcane sight›, ‹spell/clairaudience/clairvoyance›, ‹spell/dispel magic›, ‹spell/nondetection›, ‹spell/seek thoughts›, *twilight knife*
- 4th-‹spell/minor creation›, ‹spell/moonstruck›, *wandering star motes*

- 6th-‹spell/greater dispel magic›, ‹spell/legend lore›, *true seeing,* ‹spell/mage's lucubration›
- 7th-‹spell/greater teleport›, ‹spell/simulacrum›, ‹spell/vision›

- 9th-‹spell/foresight›, ‹spell/mage's disjunction›, ‹spell/time stop›

water.name: Water
description: ## Water
‹SOURCE Advanced Player's Guide/143›
The water elementalist draws magic from the ocean depths. His power is fluid as well, crushing foes in mighty waves or wearing them down through timeless erosion.

::ab[Water Supremacy (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost-power-roll passive="You gain a +2 enhancement bonus on Swim skill checks. This bonus increases by +1 for every five wizard levels you possess. In addition, you can ‹misc/hold your breath› for a number of rounds equal to four times your Constitution score before you must start making checks to avoid ‹rule/drowning›. At 10th level, you gain a swim speed equal to your base speed." imp5="The bonus becomes +3." imp10="The bonus becomes +4." imp15="The bonus becomes +5." imp20="The bonus becomes +6. In addition, whenever you make a Swim skill check, assume the roll resulted in a natural 20."}

::ab[Cold Blast (Su)]{l=1 icon=aura standard="You can unleash a blast of freezing cold. This blast deals 1d6 points of cold damage + 1 point for every two wizard levels you possess to all creatures within 5 feet of you and ‹misc/stagger«s› them for 1 round. A successful Reflex save negates the staggered effect and halves the damage. The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Wave (Su)]{l=8 icon=line standard="You can create a wave of water that starts at your location and moves 30 feet per round away from you in a direction of your choosing. Creatures struck by the wave might be knocked down and pushed away. Make a caster level check against the CMD of each creature struck. If you succeed, the creature is knocked ‹rule/prone›. If your check exceeds the CMD of the creature by 5 or more, it is carried with the wave during the wave's movement this round. You receive a +5 bonus on this check if the creature shares its space with the wave at the start of your turn (when the wave moves). Creatures carried in this way can move out of the wave on their turn if they succeed on a Strength check (DC = 10 + 1/2 your caster level + your Intelligence modifier), but they cannot breathe while sharing space with the wave. The wave is up to 20 feet high and up to 5 feet long for each wizard level you possess. Nonmagical fires hit by the wave are automatically extinguished. Magical fire effects are unaffected." useInc="2~wizard" useUnit=round}
::h3[Ice School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Air or Water.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *cold blast* and *wave powers* of the water school.

::ab[Freezing Shards (Su)]{l=1 icon=aura standard="You can cause shards of ice to burst forth from the ground around you. The shards deal 1d4 points of piercing damage and 1d6 points of cold damage + 1 point for every 2 wizard levels you have to all creatures within 5 feet of you. A successful Reflex save (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) negates the piercing damage and halves the cold damage. The shards remain until the beginning of your next turn; during this time, the area is ‹rule/difficult terrain›. You are not affected by your own freezing shards and can move through the area normally." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Icy Shackles (Su)]{l=8 icon=melee standard="You can make a melee touch attack against a foe to conjure icy restraints around its ankles, reducing its base speed to 5 feet and dealing it an amount of cold damage at the beginning of its turn equal to your Intelligence modifier. The manacles have AC 10, hardness 0, and 30 hit points, and a creature can destroy them with a successful Strength check (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) as a standard action. The icy shackles melt away after 1 minute." useMod=Int}
::h3[Mud School]{jl}
‹SOURCE Elemental Master's Handbook/21›
**Associated School:** Earth or Water.
**Replacement Powers:** The following school powers replace the *cold blast* and *wave* powers of the water school.

::ab[Hindering Muck (Su)]{l=1 icon=cone standard="You can create a 15-foot cone of sticky mud. Any creature in the cone must succeed at a Reflex save (DC = 10 + half your wizard level + your Intelligence modifier) or become ‹rule/entangled› for 1 round for every wizard level you have. An affected creature can take a standard action that provokes attacks of opportunity to wipe off enough of the mud to remove the entangled condition. Jumping into a large body of water or being subject to an effect that creates a substantial amount of water (such as a casting of ‹spell/create water›) removes all the mud on a creature." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Mud Missile (Su)]{l=8 icon=zap standard="You can fling a ball of hardened mud at a foe within 30 feet. If you succeed at a ranged touch attack, the mud slams into the target's face, dealing 1d6 points of bludgeoning damage for every 2 wizard levels you have and ‹misc/blind«ing› the target for 1d4 rounds. A creature can remove the blinding mud from itself or another creature with a successful Strength check (DC = 10 + half your wizard level) as a standard action." useInc="2~wizard"}

wood.name: Wood
description: ## Wood
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/88›
Wood represents flexibility, warmth, wind, generosity, cooperation, and idealism. Practitioners of this elemental magic often resemble druids in character and in the use of their magic.

::ab[Wood Magic]{icon=learn s2=Entangle s3="Tree shape" s4="Plant growth" s5="Command plants" s6="Tree stride" s7=Liveoak s8="Transmute metal to wood" s9="Control plants" note="Add these spells to your wizard spell list at the listed spell level."}

::ab[Flexible Enhancement (Su)]{l=1 icon=def passive="A master of the wood element is able to bend like bamboo when stressed and snap back into place. You gain a +1 enhancement bonus to your Dexterity, Constitution, or Wisdom ability score. You can change this bonus to a new ability score when you prepare spells." imp5="The bonus becomes +2." imp10="The bonus becomes +3." imp15="The bonus becomes +4." imp20="The bonus becomes +5. In addition, this bonus applies to two of these ability scores of your choice."}

::ab[Splintered Spear (Su)]{l=1 icon=zap standard="You can create a wooden ‹eq-weapon/shortspear› appropriate to your size, which hurls itself as a ranged attack against one target within 100 feet (range penalties apply), using your Intelligence modifier as an attack bonus instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier. The spear deals normal damage according to its size, plus your Intelligence modifier, then breaks into countless splinters; the target takes 1 point of ‹rule/bleed› damage each round on its turn." imp6="The spear gains a +1 enhancement bonus and the bleed damage increases to 2." imp12="The spear's bonus becomes +2 and the bleed damage increases to 3." imp18="The spear's bonus becomes +3 and the bleed damage increases to 4." useMod3=Int}

::ab[Cooperative Defense (Su)]{l=8 icon=protect immediate="Whenever a spell or effect targets you and one or more allies within 30 feet, you can use this ability to allow your allies to use your saving throw against the effect in place of their own. Each ally must make this choice individually before the rolls are made." useInc="4~wizard~8"}
